title: pavonia patens var. tomentosa
intro: Pavonia patens var. tomentosa, also known as the woolly rock rose or hairy swampmallow, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, which are attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of the plant include wound healing, reduction of fever and inflammation, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as skin conditions, infections, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or salves, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
